intent:
PREMISE CONTRACT (story 28)
  one_sentence_event:    The narrator meets mother at the station; mother hands over a small old letter the narrator wrote as a child and had forgotten; the narrator opens it, recognizes their own handwriting but cannot understand the words, until mother explains it is not a new letter but one from long ago — the narrator finally understands and promises not to forget, and mother laughs softly.
  change_of_state:       at s0 the narrator stands waiting and knows nothing of the letter; at the closer the letter has changed owners (mother → narrator), the narrator has recognized their own past handwriting, the words have become legible through mother's explanation, and mother has laughed quietly in acknowledgement.
  scene_novelty_claim:   I am NOT writing a bookstore / narrator_home / park story. Scene is the station (駅).
  anchor_novelty_claim:  Anchor is 手紙 (letter). Last appeared as ANCHOR: never — it was a passing object once, never the carried piece.
  closer_shape_claim:    Closer shape: action — "母は静かに笑いました。" (noun-WA-adv-NI-verb-mashita). NOT a noun-pile, NOT a 「Nは Adj です」 mirror, NOT a と言いました mirror of story 27.
  why_not_filler:        mother — gives the letter, explains it, reacts to the narrator's apology; narrator — asks, opens, reads, recognizes, apologizes; letter — gift then puzzle then recognition then resolution. Every character/object earns multiple slots through verb-driven turns.
  obligations_absorbed:  must-reinforce W00142 分かる (s5 quoted 分からなかった, s7 plain 分かりました), W00143 まだ (s5 inside the quoted thought), W00144 忘れる (s7 もう忘れません); must-reinforce-grammar N5_nakatta via 「言葉がまだ分からなかった」と思いました (s5 inner thought, plain past negative), naturally absorbing the R1 grammar window; must-introduce N5_na_adj_neg via 「新しい手紙じゃありません」 in s6, absorbed inside mother's explanation — not bolted on.

FORBIDDEN THIS STORY:
  scene_class    (last 3 stories): bookstore, narrator_home, park
  anchor_object  (last 5 stories): ノート, 傘, 本, 石, 絵
  closer_shape   (last 3 stories): noun-NI-noun-WA-noun-noun, noun-WA-noun-WO-noun-noun-NO-noun-NI-noun, noun-WA-noun-noun-TO-noun
  opening_seqs   (last 3 stories' first 3 content tokens):
    - 弟 / 本屋 / 行きました
    - 友達 / 家 / 来ました
    - 友達 / 二人 / 公園
  (All four zones honored — see opening 夕方/駅/待ちました, anchor 手紙, station scene, closer 母は静かに笑いました.)

sentences.jp: 夕方、駅で母を待ちました。
sentences.en: In the evening, I waited for my mother at the station.

sentences.jp: 母は私に小さい古い手紙を渡しました。
sentences.en: My mother handed me a small old letter.

sentences.jp: 「これは何ですか」と聞きました。
母は「あなたが書いた手紙ですよ」と答えました。
sentences.en: My mother answered, "It's a letter you wrote."

sentences.jp: 手紙を開けて、ゆっくり読みました。
sentences.en: I opened the letter and read it slowly.

sentences.jp: 字は私の字でしたが、「言葉がまだ分からなかった」と思いました。
sentences.en: The handwriting was mine, but I thought, "I still didn't understand the words."

sentences.jp: 母は「新しい手紙じゃありません。小さい頃の手紙です」と言いました。
sentences.en: My mother said, "It isn't a new letter. It's a letter from when you were small."

sentences.jp: 「分かりました。もう忘れません」と言いました。
sentences.en: "I understand. I won't forget again," I said.

sentences.jp: 母は静かに笑いました。
